spec_match.rationale: Lays out the six Clear-Site-Data directives plus the wildcard with correct camelCase spellings, includes `executionContexts`, and now reflects the spec rule that `cache` and `*` also clear `prefetchCache`.

interactive.rationale: Click any token tile to flip it on/off; the header builds live and the impact matrix recolours. Well past static-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises all six directives plus the wildcard, an impact matrix, and a pre-v138 vs v138 logout comparison — the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Wildcard, individual, combined token selections, and the implicit cache→prefetchCache promotion are all live. Cross-origin scoping and active-prerender skip behavior are covered by sibling concepts rather than this compact token matrix.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links to the correct ChromeStatus feature 4755745652080640.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables only, click-toggle pattern uses the proper inverse (background var(--text-black) with color var(--bg-ivory)), the duplicate pseudo-element badge was removed, and the page remains an interactive builder rather than a static card.

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148. Before the fix, selecting only `cache` rendered HTTP cache as cleared but left `prefetchCache` as kept. After the fix, selecting `cache` keeps the header as `Clear-Site-Data: "cache"`, marks both HTTP cache and prefetch cache cleared, and announces the implicit promotion. The wildcard clears every row including `executionContexts`; direct `prefetchCache` selection still only clears the prefetch cache row. The page had no console errors.
